codeReview.summary: The worker thoroughly verified all four governance assertions. The transcript shows comprehensive verification of issue templates, PR template, GitHub Actions workflows, and git hooks. All verification commands executed successfully with proper exit codes. The worker correctly identified that this is a verification-only feature (no code changes) and that the secret-scan failures are pre-existing infrastructure issues unrelated to governance artifacts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly identified that npm run secret-scan reports false positives in .factory/validation/ directory due to personal paths in validation reports. This is a known infrastructure issue that should be documented in AGENTS.md or mission documentation to prevent confusion in future validation tasks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows worker ran: npm run secret-scan, and noted: 'The secret-scan issue is a pre-existing infrastructure problem with .factory/validation/ reports containing personal paths - this is not related to the governance artifacts I'm verifying.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill (mission-worker-base + worker) provides clear guidance on verification-only features. Worker correctly understood that no code changes were needed and that the task was to verify existing artifacts. This procedural clarity worked well.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker used Skill tool twice (mission-worker-base, worker), then proceeded with verification steps without attempting to modify any governance artifacts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No services were started or needed for this governance verification task. The commands used (git ls-files, ls, cat, npm test, npm run typecheck, npm run secret-scan) are all read-only verification commands properly documented in .factory/services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript shows all commands were read-only: git ls-files hooks/, ls -la .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/, cat .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md, grep for secrets in workflows, npm test, npm run typecheck, npm run secret-scan.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker demonstrated understanding of OSS governance requirements by verifying: (1) Issue templates use YAML format with proper fields (version, platform, reproduction steps), (2) PR template includes type classification, skills section, and release hygiene checkboxes, (3) GitHub Actions workflows use GitHub context variables (no hardcoded secrets), (4) Git hooks are executable bash scripts with proper set -euo pipefail. This knowledge could be documented as a governance verification checklist in .factory/library/ if it existed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker read and verified: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml (11 form fields), .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.yml (6 form fields), .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md (type checkboxes, skills section, release hygiene), .github/workflows/release-readiness.yml (3 jobs: secret-scan, quick-checks, full-checks), .github/workflows/skills-only.yml (skill+source mixing prevention), hooks/pre-commit, hooks/pre-push, hooks/pre-merge-commit

summary: Governance verification completed successfully. All four assertions pass: (1) VAL-GOV-001: Issue templates exist with bug_report.yml and feature_request.yml providing comprehensive structured forms; (2) VAL-GOV-002: PR template exists with type classification (skill/fix/simplification/release), skills section, and release hygiene checkboxes; (3) VAL-GOV-003: GitHub Actions workflows (release-readiness.yml, skills-only.yml) use GitHub context variables and environment variables - no hardcoded secrets detected; (4) VAL-GOV-004: Git hooks (pre-commit, pre-merge-commit, pre-push) are tracked in git and use proper bash scripting patterns. Worker followed verification procedure correctly, identified pre-existing secret-scan false positives as unrelated to governance, and confirmed all tests and typecheck pass.
